title: SAIL Position
point: SAIL occupies a pre-eminent position in the Indian Iron and Steel Industry.

title: Product Range
point: SAIL produces hundreds of plain carbon steel items.

title: Marketing Network
point: SAIL markets products largely through a network of stockyards.

title: By-Products
point: SAIL plants produce various by-products and coal chemicals.

title: Steel Types
point: SAIL supplies alloy steels and stainless steel.

title: External Relations
point: Almost every SAIL activity requires interaction with external agencies.

title: PSU Obligation
point: SAIL has high obligations because it is a Public Sector Company.

title: Government Framework
point: SAIL operations are influenced by government policies rules and regulations.

title: Government Approval
point: Government approvals were mandatory in most activities for many years.

title: Steel Pricing
point: Pricing of steel was decontrolled.

title: MOU
point: MOU stands for Memorandum of Understanding.

title: MOU Impact
point: MOU with the Department of Steel increased SAIL's functional autonomy.

title: MOU Responsibility
point: MOU also increased SAIL's responsibility.

title: Distribution Policy
point: Revision of steel distribution policy increased SAIL's autonomy.

title: Customer Contact
point: Policy changes brought SAIL into more direct contact with customers.

title: Business Environment
point: Industrial policy influences SAIL's business environment.

title: Fiscal Policy
point: Fiscal policy affects SAIL operations.

title: Foreign Trade Policy
point: Foreign Trade policy affects SAIL's performance.

title: Licensing Policy
point: Licensing policy influences SAIL activities.

title: Plan Targets
point: Plan targets affect SAIL's business environment.

title: Employment Reservation
point: Reservation in employment affects SAIL activities.

title: Pollution Control
point: Pollution control policies influence SAIL performance.

title: Peripheral Development
point: Peripheral development policies affect SAIL operations.

title: Area of Interaction
point: Investment in capacity creation is a major area of interaction.

title: Area of Interaction
point: Organising production is a major area of interaction.

title: Area of Interaction
point: Marketing of products is a major area of interaction.

title: Area of Interaction
point: Interface with Government and Ministry is a major area of interaction.

title: Funding Agencies
point: Various banks act as funding agencies.

title: MECON
point: MECON is a design and consultancy organization associated with SAIL.

title: M N Dastur
point: M N Dastur and Company is a consultancy organization linked with SAIL.

title: Foreign Collaboration
point: Foreign collaborations support capacity creation activities.

title: BHEL
point: BHEL is mentioned among important external organizations.

title: Equipment Suppliers
point: Foreign equipment suppliers support capacity creation.

title: Input Sources
point: Coal India Limited is a key source of inputs.

title: Power Supply
point: Public sector power plants provide essential inputs.

title: Mineral Sources
point: Public and private mineral companies supply inputs.

title: Refractories
point: Suppliers of refractories support production.

title: Spare Parts
point: Suppliers of spare parts are important external agencies.

title: Petroleum Inputs
point: Oil and petroleum companies are production-support agencies.

title: SBI Role
point: State Bank of India provides cash and credit support.

title: Imported Inputs
point: Foreign suppliers provide coking coal and refractories.

title: Employment Exchanges
point: Employment exchanges support workforce recruitment.

title: Campus Recruitment
point: Universities are sources for campus recruitment.

title: Professional Institutions
point: Professional institutions support recruitment.

title: Employee Representation
point: Executive and non-executive representative organizations interact with SAIL.

title: Labour Courts
point: Labour courts are part of workforce-related interactions.

title: Tribunals
point: Tribunals are involved in workforce matters.

title: Transportation
point: Railways are critical transportation partners.

title: Road Transport
point: Road transport agencies support logistics.

title: Shipping Lines
point: Shipping lines handle imported inputs.

title: Customer Groups
point: Foundries are important customer groups.

title: Customer Groups
point: Re-rollers are important customer groups.

title: Customer Groups
point: Tube makers are important customer groups.

title: Customer Groups
point: Wire drawing units are important customer groups.

title: Customer Groups
point: Automobile manufacturers are major buyers.

title: Customer Groups
point: SSICs are listed among customer groups.

title: Customer Groups
point: Fertilizer dealers are customer groups.

title: Conversion Agents
point: Conversion agents operate for mild steel and stainless steel.

title: Regulating Agency
point: CCI and E is a regulating agency.

title: Regulating Agency
point: DGTD is a regulating agency.

title: State Departments
point: Directorates of Industries regulate certain activities.

title: Sales Tax
point: Sales Tax departments are external regulatory agencies.

title: Weights and Measures
point: Weights and Measures departments regulate operations.

title: Local Bodies
point: Municipalities are regulatory stakeholders.

title: Dependence
point: SAIL remains substantially dependent on external agencies.

title: Infrastructure Support
point: Railways provide critical infrastructural support.

title: CIL Support
point: CIL is essential for production continuity.

title: Vendors
point: Vendors are important production-support stakeholders.

title: Consumer Reach
point: Railways help deliver materials to consumers.

title: PSU
point: SAIL is a Public Sector Unit.

title: Parliamentary Oversight
point: SAIL is answerable to various government agencies.

title: Consultative Committee
point: SAIL Consultative Committee reviews company functioning.

title: Public Undertakings Committee
point: Committee on Public Undertakings examines SAIL activities.

title: Public Accounts Committee
point: Public Accounts Committee reviews accountability aspects.

title: Official Language Committee
point: Committee on Official Languages visits SAIL units.

title: CAG Audit
point: CAG audits SAIL from time to time.

title: Coke Ovens
point: Director of Explosives regulates transfer and use of coke oven products.

title: Mechanical Maintenance
point: Chief Boiler Inspector is linked with mechanical maintenance.

title: Mechanical Maintenance
point: Factory Inspector is linked with mechanical maintenance.

title: Mechanical Maintenance
point: Inspector of Weights and Measures is linked with maintenance regulation.

title: Electrical Maintenance
point: Chief Electrical Inspector is a key authority.

title: Electrical Maintenance
point: State Electricity Board interacts with electrical maintenance.

title: Traffic
point: Divisional Superintendent Railways is an external authority.

title: Traffic
point: Controller of Purchase and Stores is involved in traffic-related coordination.

title: Traffic
point: General Manager SE Railway Kolkata is an important authority.

title: Safety Engineering
point: DIHS is associated with safety engineering.

title: Safety Engineering
point: State Labour Department supports safety oversight.

title: Safety Engineering
point: National Safety Council is an important stakeholder.

title: Pollution Control
point: Central Pollution Control authorities regulate pollution issues.

title: Pollution Control
point: State Pollution Control authorities regulate pollution issues.

title: Personnel
point: Registrar of Trade Union interacts with personnel functions.

title: Personnel
point: Assistant Labour Commissioner is a personnel-related authority.

title: Customer Focus
point: SAIL increasingly emphasizes direct customer interaction.

title: Customer Dialogue
point: Holding dialogues with customers is a relationship-building method.

title: Customer Visits
point: SAIL visits customer offices and factories.

title: Plant Visits
point: Customers are invited to visit SAIL plants.

title: Interaction Level
point: Customer interaction occurs from highest to branch level.

title: Quality Improvement
point: Customer interaction leads to quality improvement.

title: Product Development
point: Customer interaction supports product development.

title: Timely Deliveries
point: Customer interaction helps improve delivery performance.

title: Product Mix
point: Customer interaction aids product-mix rationalization.

title: Economic Growth
point: SAIL contributes to industrial and infrastructural development.

title: Remote Areas
point: SAIL supplies steel to remote regions.

title: Backward Areas
point: SAIL supplies steel to backward areas.

title: Strategic Areas
point: SAIL supplies steel to strategic locations.

title: Employment Generation
point: SAIL generates employment opportunities.

title: Model Employer
point: SAIL provides workforce amenities expected from a model employer.

title: Profit Criterion
point: Profit alone does not reflect SAIL's total contribution.

title: Workforce Expectations
point: SAIL seeks workforce contribution toward company objectives.

title: Participative Management
point: Participative management is the preferred approach in SAIL.

title: Executive Body
point: Executives have an association.

title: Non-Executive Body
point: Non-executives are represented through unions.

title: Negotiating Body
point: There is one negotiating body at all-India level.

title: Unit Unions
point: Each SAIL unit has separate unions and associations.

title: Corporate Negotiation
point: Unit unions do not directly negotiate with corporate management.

title: Agreement Adoption
point: National agreements are adopted at unit level through negotiations.

title: Executive Process
point: Executive agreements are directly implemented across units.

title: Infrastructure
point: Coal power and rail movement are critical infrastructure inputs.

title: Production Continuity
point: Infrastructure support is vital for uninterrupted production.

title: Demand Gap
point: Infrastructure availability lags behind demand.

title: Monitoring Authority
point: Cabinet Committee on Industrial Infrastructure monitors critical inputs.

title: Coking Coal Requirement
point: One tonne of saleable steel requires about one tonne of coking coal.

title: Electricity Requirement
point: One tonne of saleable steel requires about 500 units of electricity.

title: Rail Traffic Requirement
point: One tonne of saleable steel requires about five tonnes of rail traffic.

title: Bulk Consumer
point: Steel plants are among the largest consumers of infrastructure facilities.

title: Coking Coal Consumption
point: Nearly all domestic coking coal is consumed by steel plants.

title: DVC Power Share
point: Nearly one-third of DVC power is consumed by SAIL plants in Eastern Region.

title: Rail Traffic Share
point: About 15 percent of total railway traffic is linked to SAIL plants.

title: Railways Stakeholder
point: Indian Railways is a very important stakeholder for SAIL.

title: Blast Furnace Fuel
point: Hard coke acts as fuel and reductant in blast furnaces.

title: Global Standard
point: Coke with less than 10 percent ash is preferred worldwide.

title: Indian Coal Quality
point: Indian coking coal often contains nearly 25 percent ash.

title: Coal Washing
point: Indian coking coal is washed to around 17 percent ash.

title: Coal Reserves
point: Coking coal reserves in India are limited.

title: Coalfield Location
point: Major reserves are in Jharkhand and Bengal coalfields.

title: Supply Constraint
point: Coal availability is not keeping pace with steel demand.

title: Major Supplier
point: Coal India Limited is the major supplier of coking coal.

title: Power Need
point: Steel plants require large amounts of power.

title: DVC Dependence
point: Steel plants depend heavily on utilities like DVC.

title: Grid Requirement
point: Rolling operations create sudden load surges requiring a large grid system.
